hypothesis: Fine-tuning the transformer model on a small set of experimentally measured ATR-IR mixture spectra that deliberately include compound pairs with strong intermolecular interactions (e.g., hydrogen-bond donors paired with acceptors, such as acids with amines) will disproportionately improve prediction accuracy for minority-concentration components compared to majority-concentration components, because the primary source of error for low-concentration targets is nonlinear spectral deviation from the linear superposition assumption rather than signal weakness per se, and this hypothesis is testable by comparing Top-10 accuracy gains at 10% versus 90% concentration levels before and after such interaction-focused fine-tuning against a control fine-tuned on non-interacting compound pairs.
